title: Learning the natural history of human disease with generative transformers
title_note: Author Correction to the original article (DOI: 10.1038/s41586-025-09529-3). Correction fixes a typographical minus-sign error in the Methods exponential waiting-time model formula; scientific conclusions are unaffected.
methods: GPT-2 transformer with continuous age encoding
data_sources: UK Biobank (primary care, hospital admissions, cancer registry, death registry, self-report)
Danish Register of Causes of Death
core_conclusion: Delphi-2M, a GPT-2-based generative transformer trained on 402,799 UK Biobank participants, predicts rates for more than 1,000 ICD-10 diseases simultaneously from electronic health record sequences, matching or outperforming single-disease clinical risk models. External validation on 1.93 million Danish registry individuals (no retraining) confirms cross-national transferability. Generative sampling produces synthetic trajectories that retain 97% of the real model's discriminative performance, enabling privacy-preserving AI development. SHAP analysis reveals temporally structured comorbidity clusters and quantifies how past diagnoses modulate future disease rates over years.
why_it_matters: First population-scale generative model spanning the full ICD-10 spectrum; cross-national validation without parameter changes; synthetic trajectories achieve near-real AUC enabling privacy-safe AI; SHAP interpretability maps temporal comorbidity structure; transparent bias characterization of data-source missingness.
why_nature: Extends LLM paradigm to health records at 2M-person scale; independent Danish replication (1.93M, no retraining) rare in clinical AI; 20-year disease burden forecasting informs healthcare planning; open code (GitHub) and reproducible framework align with Nature open-science expectations.
added_value_to_my_research: Spatial embedding in Delphi-2M links neighbourhood healthcare accessibility (R2SFCA) to disease incidence trajectories
Synthetic Delphi trajectories aggregate into future demand maps for geographically targeted facility-location optimization
SHAP comorbidity matrix provides temporal hazard ratios comparable to my spatiotemporal kernel density estimation framework
Place-stratified AUC analysis of Delphi reveals how primary care access gaps propagate into EHR-based AI fairness
Multi-task token extension blueprint informs multi-source data fusion in geospatial foundation model fine-tuning
